metrics.notes: Periodic trunk-recorder Control Channel Decode Rates value. This is the normal msg/sec metric discussed by TR users.
Percent of periodic Control Channel Decode Rates samples that were 0 msg/sec; lower is better.
Count of per-frequency Control Channel Message Decode Rate samples. These are short-interval source samples, not warning log lines.
Percent of per-frequency Control Channel Message Decode Rate samples reporting 0/sec.
High counts can indicate weak/unstable control-channel reception or incorrect channel list.
Count of Concluding Recorded Call lines.
Calls where trunk-recorder reported no transmission audio was recorded.
Calls dropped because all configured recorders for the needed source/system were busy.
Maximum sampled trunk-recorder CPU percent in this health window. 100% is one fully saturated core.
Maximum resident memory used by trunk-recorder. This is real memory, unlike virtual address size.
Raspberry Pi vcgencmd throttling flags when available.
Nonzero means trunk-recorder reported a source stopped receiving samples.
Usually points to source min/max coverage or SDR count/configuration.
